task_completion.description: Weasis is open and a DICOM image is loaded in the viewer.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Weasis application window is visible with a medical image (e.g., X-Ray Angiography) displayed in the main viewing area.

task_completion.description: The 'Hot Iron' LUT is applied to the DICOM image.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The image in the viewer is colorized using a thermal color scale (black to red to orange to yellow to white) instead of grayscale. The UI may also show 'Hot Iron' selected in a LUT dropdown or toolbar.

task_completion.description: The export or save dialog is opened to save the colorized view.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A dialog box for exporting or saving the image is visible over the Weasis interface.

task_completion.description: The export path is set to ~/DICOM/exports/colorized_ct.png.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The file path or name field in the export dialog shows '/home/ga/DICOM/exports/colorized_ct.png' or '~/DICOM/exports/colorized_ct.png'.

task_completion.description: The export format is set to PNG.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The file type or format dropdown in the export dialog is set to 'PNG' or '.png'.

integrity.description: The agent used the Weasis GUI to apply the LUT and export the image.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show interaction with Weasis menus, toolbars, and dialogs rather than terminal commands executing image processing tools (like ImageMagick).

integrity.description: The export dialog belongs to the Weasis application.
integrity.visual_evidence: The export dialog matches the Java/Swing UI style of Weasis and is not from a different image editor.

integrity.description: The agent did not bypass the GUI by using a script to generate the output.
integrity.visual_evidence: There are no terminal windows showing python scripts or shell commands directly creating the 'colorized_ct.png' file.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The expected output path is exactly '/home/ga/DICOM/exports/colorized_ct.png'. The required LUT is 'Hot Iron', which applies a thermal color scale (black-red-orange-yellow-white) to the image. The exported file must be a PNG image.
